name: Layout Areas
description: Composable visualizations for chat responses and reports

content.title: Layout Areas
content.content:
# Layout Areas

MeshWeaver provides layout areas - reusable visualization components that can be composed into dashboards, reports, and chat responses.

## What Are Layout Areas?

Layout areas are addressable UI components:

```
@app/Claims/SummaryKPI         # A KPI card
@app/Claims/TrendChart         # A chart
@app/Claims/RecentClaimsTable  # A data grid
```

Because they're addressable, they can be:
- Composed into dashboards
- Embedded in AI chat responses
- Reused across applications
- Updated independently

## Component Types

| Component | Description | Use Case |
|-----------|-------------|----------|
| **Tables** | Data grids with sorting/filtering | Claims list, document index |
| **Charts** | Bar, line, pie visualizations | Trends, distributions |
| **KPIs** | Key performance indicators | Status summaries |
| **Forms** | Data entry interfaces | Submission intake |
| **Cards** | Information summaries | Record overviews |

## Composition

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "Layout Areas"
        A[Claims KPI]
        B[Trend Chart]
        C[Claims Table]
    end

    subgraph "Composed Views"
        D[Dashboard]
        E[Report]
        F[Chat Response]
    end

    A --> D
    B --> D
    C --> D

    A --> E
    B --> E

    A --> F
    C --> F
```

## AI Chat Integration

When AI responds to questions, it can embed layout areas:

**User**: "Show me the claims summary"

**AI Response**:
> Here's the current claims overview:
>
> [Embedded: @app/Claims/SummaryKPI]
>
> And the recent claims:
>
> [Embedded: @app/Claims/RecentClaimsTable]

The user sees live, interactive visualizations - not static text.

## Live Data

Layout areas are connected to the data mesh:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant Area as Layout Area
    participant Mesh as Data Mesh
    participant Source as Data Source

    User->>Area: View dashboard
    Area->>Mesh: Subscribe to data
    Mesh->>Source: Connect to source
    Source->>Mesh: Initial data
    Mesh->>Area: Render with data
    Area->>User: Display visualization

    Note over Source: Data changes

    Source->>Mesh: Update notification
    Mesh->>Area: Delta update
    Area->>User: Refresh automatically
```

## Report Generation

Compose layout areas into documents:

```yaml
report:
  name: Monthly Claims Summary
  sections:
    - header: "Executive Summary"
      areas:
        - @app/Claims/ExecutiveKPIs
    - header: "Claims Trends"
      areas:
        - @app/Claims/MonthlyTrendChart
        - @app/Claims/ByLineOfBusiness
    - header: "Details"
      areas:
        - @app/Claims/TopClaimsTable
```

## Benefits

| Traditional Approach | Layout Areas Approach |
|---------------------|----------------------|
| Build each dashboard from scratch | Compose from reusable components |
| Static reports | Live, updating visualizations |
| Separate chat and BI | Unified experience |
| Code changes for new views | Configuration-driven |

## Consistency

Because layout areas are reusable:
- Same KPI definition used everywhere
- Metrics calculated consistently
- Updates propagate to all usages
- No drift between dashboard and report
